name: Rasagolla
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring one gallon of milk to a boil. When boiling, add one cup of either white vinegar or lemon juice and turn off the stove.
2. Allow the mixture to cool until it separates into whey and curd.
3. Pour the mixture through a colander to leave only the paneer/curd.
4. Leave the curd in the strainer until it is cold and dry (at least an hour, or overnight).
5. Process the curd in a food processor for one minute until it is soft but not sticky.
6. Form small balls from the curd; vinegar typically yields about 80 to 100 rasagollas.
7. Bring one cup sugar and three cups water to a boil in a pressure cooker.
8. Place 20-25 rasagollas in the syrup and turn off the heat, placing the cover on the pressure cooker.
9. When the pressure cooker begins to whistle, wait for a couple of minutes before turning it off.
10. Once the pressure cooker has depressurized, remove the cover and repeat the process with the remaining rasagollas. Do not use the same sugar syrup more than once.
